name: Skill Challenge: Beat 10 — Cave Entrance Preparation

text.content:
Skill Challenge: Beat 10 — Cave Entrance Preparation

Act: Act 2 | Difficulty: Moderate (DC 13)

Successes Needed: 4 | Failures Allowed: 2

Overview

The Echoing Caves are tidal. The party must time their approach correctly, gather equipment, and prepare for the descent into a passage that fills with seawater when the tide rises. Lysandre the Fishwife (if the party spoke with her) can advise on timing and the carved figure of Líf inside. The party has a narrow window between tides.

Running the Skill Challenge

Each character takes a turn attempting one skill check. On their turn, a player describes how they're helping the party and rolls the appropriate skill against DC 13. A success adds to the group's total; a failure adds to the failure count. If the party reaches 4 successes before 2 failures, they succeed overall.

Suggested Skills:

  • Athletics
  • Acrobatics or Stealth
  • Arcana or Investigation
  • Perception, Survival, or Insight
  • Persuasion, Intimidation, or Deception

Players may also suggest creative skill uses — reward good roleplaying with advantage or a lower DC.

Complications

The tide window is narrowing. Scouts and preparation tests must be made quickly. Low tide lasts approximately two hours. Characters must assess the entrance, secure ropes, and prepare for the descent.

  • Reason: Estimate the tidal schedule from wave patterns and rock staining (moderately difficult).
  • Agility: Climb the slick rock face to secure an anchor point for the group's descent rope.
  • Intuition: Read the Vanir carvings near the entrance to understand what the passage is for.
  • Presence: Rally the group's focus and calm nerves before entering a tight, tide-threatened space.

Later Rounds

Inside the cave, the passage narrows and the sound of water amplifies strangely — the caves are beginning to fill. The party must move efficiently through the carved stone corridor toward the figure of Líf before the water rises.

  • Might: Force a stone door or squeeze through a collapsed section of passage.
  • Agility: Move through rising ankle-deep water without losing footing.
  • Intuition: Find the hand-carved figure of Líf and determine how to interact with the water from her hands.
  • Reason: Recall knowledge of Vanir ritual sites to understand what the figure triggers.

Outcomes

Total Success R1: Ropes secured, tidal schedule confirmed, carvings partially understood. The party enters with time to spare and a sense of the cave's purpose. +1 to Round 2 tests from preparation.
Partial Success R1: Ropes secured. Tidal estimate uncertain — may have less time than believed.
Failure R1: The party scrambles. The first characters through have to improvise rope anchors under pressure. Someone starts Round 2 with the slowed condition from a cold-water stumble.

Total Success: The party reaches the figure of Líf with time to spare, understands the Vanir ritual significance, and descends safely as the tide begins rising behind them. They enter the cave complex prepared and oriented.
Partial Success: The party gets through, but wet and rushed. One hero takes 4 cold damage from a slip into deeper water.
Failure: The party scrambles through the tidal surge. At least one hero takes 8 cold damage and loses a piece of light equipment (food, torch) to the water. The passage is blocked until the next tide window (6 hours). If already at the figure, it activates incompletely.
